methods.description: This method involves the classification of species into groups based on their tolerance to pollution and the use of canonical correspondence analysis (CCA) to understand the relationship between species distribution and environmental variables.

steps.description: From the analysis of the indicator species three main groups were identified.
steps.searchable_summary: classify species into groups based on indicator species analysis

steps.description: The canonical correspondence analysis (CCA) explained 30.2% of the total variability of data in its first two axes.
steps.searchable_summary: perform canonical correspondence analysis to explain data variability

steps.description: The Monte Carlo permutation test revealed that the ordination of these axes was statistically significant (p < 0.01).
steps.searchable_summary: validate CCA results using Monte Carlo permutation test

steps.description: The CCA results revealed that axis 1 explained 17.3% of the variability highlighting the chloride environmental variable.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze CCA axis 1 for environmental variable correlation

steps.description: For axis 2 (12.9% of explained variation), the canonical coefficient showed a high correlation with BOD 5.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze CCA axis 2 for BOD 5 correlation

steps.description: The distribution of the three groups of TWINSPAN indicator species on the CCA chart revealed an evident sequence along the gradient of organic contamination and eutrophication.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize species distribution on CCA chart

methods.description: This method describes the process of collecting and preparing epilithic diatom samples from river substrates for quantitative analysis.

steps.description: Simultaneous to the collection of water samples, the epilithic diatoms were sampled from rock substrates.
steps.searchable_summary: collect epilithic diatoms from rock substrates

steps.description: The samples for quantitative analysis were taken by scrubbing each stone with a toothbrush.
steps.searchable_summary: scrub stones to collect diatom samples

steps.description: The compound sample was well mixed and an aliquot of 40 mL was separated for analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: mix and aliquot diatom sample for analysis

steps.description: This material was oxidized to clear the diatoms with potassium permanganate and concentrated hydrochloric acid.
steps.searchable_summary: oxidize diatom sample with potassium permanganate and HCl

steps.description: An aliquot (1 mL) of the cleared sample was deposited and mounted on permanent slides for further observation.
steps.searchable_summary: mount cleared diatom sample on slides

steps.description: Algae were counted using procedures described by Kobayasi and Mayama (1982).
To identify taxa to specific and infra-specific levels, epilithic diatoms were observed and photographed using an optical binocular microscope at 1000x magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: identify and photograph diatoms at 1000x magnification
